SEARCH
Package edu.yu.cs.com3810.coordinator

Class LeaderElectionRunner

java.lang.Object
edu.yu.cs.com3810.coordinator.LeaderElectionRunner
All Implemented Interfaces:
Runnable
public class LeaderElectionRunner extends Object implements Runnable
Runs the Bully Algorithm leader election protocol in a dedicated thread.

Election messages (ELECTION, OK, COORDINATOR) are published to the TopicType.ELECTION topic on the Message Bus — the same bus used for all other system communication. All coordinator nodes subscribe to this topic on startup, so every node receives every election message. Each node filters by inspecting ElectionMessage.getTargetNodeId(): directed messages (ELECTION, OK) are only acted on if targetNodeId matches the receiving node's own ID. COORDINATOR messages have a null target and are acted on by all nodes.

Protocol flow

  Node A (lower ID) detects leader failure:
    A → publishes ELECTION(target=B)      [B has higher ID]
    A → publishes ELECTION(target=C)      [C has higher ID]
    A waits electionTimeoutMs for an OK...

  Node B receives ELECTION(target=B):
    B → publishes OK(target=A)
    B starts its own LeaderElectionRunner (if not already running)

  Node A receives OK(target=A):
    A calls receiveOk() → stands down, waits for COORDINATOR

  Node B (highest alive ID, no higher nodes respond):
    B waits electionTimeoutMs, receives no OK
    B → publishes COORDINATOR(target=null)   [broadcast to all]
    B calls coordinator.receiveCoordinator(selfNodeId) locally

  All nodes receive COORDINATOR:
    Each calls coordinator.receiveCoordinator(newLeaderId)

Only one MessageBusClientRunner is needed — no direct peer TCP connections are required between coordinator nodes.

Usage: 

   LeaderElectionRunner election = new LeaderElectionRunner(
       coordinator, "node-3",
       List.of("node-1", "node-2", "node-4", "node-5"),
       busClient,
       3000
   );
   new Thread(election, "leader-election").start();

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    LeaderElectionRunner(ICoordinator coordinator, String selfNodeId, List<String> peerNodeIds, MessageBusClientRunner busClient, long electionTimeoutMs)
    Constructs a LeaderElectionRunner.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    receiveOk(String fromNodeId)
    Called by ICoordinator.receiveOk(String) when an OK message arrives on the ELECTION topic targeted at this node.
    void
    run()
    Executes the Bully Algorithm: Publish ELECTION to every peer with a higher node ID. Sleep for electionTimeoutMs waiting for an OK. If no OK received → declare self leader by publishing COORDINATOR broadcast and calling ICoordinator.receiveCoordinator(String). If OK received → stand down.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • LeaderElectionRunner

      public LeaderElectionRunner(ICoordinator coordinator, String selfNodeId, List<String> peerNodeIds, MessageBusClientRunner busClient, long electionTimeoutMs)
      Constructs a LeaderElectionRunner.
      Parameters:
      coordinator - the local coordinator node
      selfNodeId - the ID of this coordinator node
      peerNodeIds - IDs of all other nodes in the cluster
      busClient - the Message Bus client for publishing election messages
      electionTimeoutMs - ms to wait for an OK before declaring self leader

  • Method Details

    • run

      public void run()
      Executes the Bully Algorithm:
      1. Publish ELECTION to every peer with a higher node ID.
      2. Sleep for electionTimeoutMs waiting for an OK.
      3. If no OK received → declare self leader by publishing COORDINATOR broadcast and calling ICoordinator.receiveCoordinator(String).
      4. If OK received → stand down. The higher-priority node will eventually publish COORDINATOR, handled by the coordinator's ELECTION subscriber.
      Specified by:
      run in interface Runnable

    • receiveOk

      public void receiveOk(String fromNodeId)
      Called by ICoordinator.receiveOk(String) when an OK message arrives on the ELECTION topic targeted at this node. Signals this runner to stand down after the timeout elapses.
      Parameters:
      fromNodeId - the node ID of the higher-priority peer that sent OK
